DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY WILLIAM SHOPOFF APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CAROL JASINSKI & ATTY. RALPH KERSTETTER, TEL. NO. 1-415-644-4818 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT SIEGFRIED RUPPERT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Siegfried Ruppert's demurrer to both of the causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint is overruled. The order sustaining the demurrer to the original complaint stated that if "Ms. Buse files an amended complaint alleging the facts she says in her opposition memorandum that she can allege, then both of her two claims would be sufficiently alleged to withstand another demurrer if that demurrer was made on the same grounds as the current demurrer." In her first amended complaint, Ms. Buse has alleged the facts identified in her opposition to the original demurrer. Defendants were given 45 days leave to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SIEGFRIED RUPPERT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Plaintiff Patricia Buse is granted leave to amend to allege facts showing that her claims on the promissory note are not barred by: 1) the German Judgment of Divorce or the November 29, 2012 San Francisco family court order and 2) Code of Civil Procedure sections 580b and 726(a). For guidance of the parties going forward, if Ms. Buse files an amended complaint alleging the facts she says in her opposition memorandum that she can allege, then both of her two claims would be sufficiently alleged to withstand another demurrer if that demurrer was made on the same grounds as the current demurrer. In those circumstances Ms. Buse would have alleged that the pre-nuptial agreement is to be interpreted under German law and that under German law the pre-nuptial agreement does not preclude claims based on the promissory note and those claims are still maintainable by Ms. Buse after the German Judgment of Divorce. If Ms. Buse files an amended complaint conforming to her opposition memorandum, she would also be alleging that she no longer can avail herself of looking to the deed of trust which was to be security for the promissory note because of breach of an obligation to her by Mr. Ruppert and that the claims are not base on a "deficiency" as that term in used in the anti-deficiency statutes. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order in hard copy to Dept. 302 that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
